title: Protocol SIFT — Hidden Ground-Truth Rubric
description: BUILD-TIME EVAL TOOLING — not part of any hackathon submission. The operator-built answer key for ONE already-solved case, extracted from its published answer key (e.g. CFReDS leakage-answers.pdf). Kept HIDDEN from the investigator — run_blind.py never reads it into the prompt. A scorer compares a blind findings.json against this rubric to compute attack-type match, key-evidence recall, false-positive rate, and hallucination rate.

case_id.description: Identifier of the case this rubric answers (matches the case_id used by run_blind.py).

source.description: Provenance of the answer key (dataset name + URL/file the operator extracted it from). Not scored; for audit.

attack_type.description: Ground-truth classification. category is the single correct on-box SIFT category the investigator should land on; aliases capture acceptable free-form phrasings of the incident.

category.description: The correct primary category, verbatim from the 24-category on-box SIFT taxonomy.

secondary_categories.description: Other categories that legitimately apply to this multi-stage incident (partial credit if the investigator names one).

aliases.description: Acceptable plain-language descriptions of the incident (e.g. 'insider data theft / IP exfiltration via USB'). Used for fuzzy attack-type match.

key_artifacts.description: Must-find artifacts / files / registry keys that the investigation should surface (e.g. 'setupapi.dev.log USB install record', 'NTUSER.DAT UserAssist', '$MFT entry for stolen-doc.docx').

key_iocs.description: Must-find indicators of compromise: hashes, IPs, domains, URLs, device serials/VID-PID, filenames, mutexes, ransom-note names, etc.

timeline_events.description: Must-find timeline events. Prefer a timestamp + what happened (e.g. '2011-03-12 04:10Z USB mass-storage first connect').

actor_accounts.description: User/service/machine accounts (and roles) involved — the insider, compromised user, attacker-created account, etc.

exfil_or_encryption_facts.description: The impact facts: what data left and how (channel, volume, destination) for exfil cases, or what was encrypted/wiped and how (extension, ransom note, family, deleted shadow copies) for ransomware/destructive cases.

notes.description: Operator notes (e.g. red herrings, known anti-forensics, scoring caveats). Not scored.

sift_category.description: One of the 24 ON-BOX Detection-&-Analysis categories of the SIFT IR taxonomy (must mirror findings.schema.json exactly).

rubric_item.description: A scored ground-truth item. Either a plain string, or an object that lists acceptable phrasings and (optionally) where the answer key sources it.

value.description: Canonical text of the expected item.

aliases.description: Other acceptable phrasings (for fuzzy recall scoring).

expected_artifact.description: Where in the evidence this should be found (helps the scorer credit a correctly-sourced finding).

source.description: Where in the published answer key this item comes from (audit only).

timeline_item.description: A ground-truth timeline event. Plain string, or an object with an explicit timestamp.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (UTC preferred) of the event, if known.

aliases.description: Other acceptable phrasings of the event.

source.description: Where in the published answer key this event comes from (audit only).
